title: ricinus communis var. mexicanus
intro: Ricinus communis var. mexicanus, commonly known as the Mexican castor bean, is a variety of the castor oil plant that is native to Mexico and Central America. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in the production of ricinoleic acid, a fatty acid with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Ricinus communis var. mexicanus include reducing inflammation, soothing skin irritations, and supporting digestive health, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. Herbal preparations of the plant include oil extracts, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used topically or taken internally to reap its medicinal benefits.
